text: We'll turn tonight to Isaiah in chapter 41. Isaiah in chapter 41. And verse number, for the sake of time tonight, we'll just read from verse 17 down to verse 20. I will open rivers in high places, and fountains in the midst of the valleys. I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water. And we ask this in Jesus' name. Amen. The book of Isaiah is found here in the middle of the Bible. And in my country, we say Isaiah. I know here it's Isaiah. And if you were to read from verse chapter number one down to chapter number 39, you'd find much of the first part of Isaiah to be all about woe and difficulty and cursing. And then you get to chapter 40, and you find the first word of chapter 40 is the word comfort. Because that's the New Testament, some people say, when the Lord Jesus Christ will come. Comfort. And he did come. And he has come. And he has brought great comfort to men and women around the world. Here we are in Isaiah chapter 41 tonight. And the Bible has told us in the book of Isaiah that judgment is coming and that judgment will come to the people of God because they, particularly in Isaiah, have fallen into idolatry. If you read Isaiah 40 in verse 18, the Bible says this, to whom then will you liken God? Or what likeness will you compare unto him? And there are many people in the time of Isaiah who were making their own God. And the Lord says, the nations are like a bucket. And the Bible tells us that there were people who were making all kinds of gods. And then the Bible says in verse 21, have you not known? Have you not heard? Have you not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth? It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth. And so the Bible message of Isaiah really is to behold our God, to look to God. I remember one time I met someone who said, do you all have internet in England? We do. And we have taps just like everyone else does. We're a Western civilization. Water is a very basic need, isn't it? And here were people who were reduced to nothing. They had absolutely nothing. They didn't even have water. Look what they were seeking for it. They were looking for it. Can you imagine just for a moment here this evening, being in this part of the world and not having the provision of water? Having no tap to turn on, having no bottle to drink out of the fridge of cool, refreshing water. But this isn't talking about physical provision. I want to apply this spiritually. There are many people around the world today who do not have what you have. They don't have the freedom to worship God like you do. They don't have a gospel preacher like you do, who takes the Word of God and preaches it. And can I tell you, the country that I've come from is full of history of men and women like that. But today, the sad reality is, there are many people that don't have spiritual water. We have people in our church, in our video, we showed it to you, who travel 50 miles one way and 50 miles the other way on a Sunday just to come to worship with people who love the Word of God being preached as it should be preached. Can you imagine that? 50 miles one way with five children in the car, and 50 miles back, they stay all day? That's how serious people were for the Word of God. And here are people tonight, and they haven't got water, there is none, their tongue's failing for thirst, they begin to get desperate, then God hears them and steps in. And I tell you tonight, the sad reality is, we don't really ever see the Lord until we really need to see Him. Until we start to get desperate for God. Do you know the danger of living in a country like the United Kingdom, in a country like America, I'm assuming tonight, I'm a guest here, but is that we can get so comfortable with everything, we don't realize we need God. We're so used to the mechanics of Christianity even, we're so used to the blessings of coming into a meeting and going out again and doing that Sunday after Sunday, we forget one key thing, we need God. Can I tell you, in the nation of Wales, there were Christians, a group of five people in a little town called Welshpool, who were desperate for God to do something with their chapel building. And they didn't contact us first, God heard them. And that's always the way it works, isn't it? You may be here tonight, and you may need to know this, I need to know this, listen, what we really need is God to do something, not us. I, the Lord. Listen, when man fails, when everything's gone, listen, this is what happens in the time of Isaiah, he hears. That's what we need, isn't it? That's what we need. He hears them. And notice, look at verse 17. I will hear them, and I will not forsake them. What a wonderful verse. God hasn't finished with America. God hasn't finished with the United Kingdom. He's sitting on the cloud, as it were, of heaven, the throne room of heaven, and He's hearing His children pray, even tonight, revive us again, and He's moving. That's the Lord I know. He says, I will be with thee. No man will be able to stand against. Can you imagine being Joshua in Joshua's shoes? We've seen it in our little bit part of the vineyard. God has done impossible things in our part of the world. There was a man a number of years ago who came from a little town about 25 miles or so from where we are, about 40 minutes away. His name was Robert Germain Thomas. You may have heard of him. He had a burden to go into the Lord's work. He was ordained as a minister of the gospel and that day it was a very Christian place where he was living in Wales at that time and minister came to him one day and said, well, where are you going to go? What town of Wales are you going to be? He said, I'd like to take the gospel to the outermost parts of the world and he went. I believe the first place he went to was China. He arrived in China and as he was there in China within a year his wife who he just married had passed away. She died. He then felt like, how can I go on? And so he stopped working in the ministry and he got a job with languages, part of the Chinese office there. He was a translator and as he was there he came across people who were making their way across the sea from North Korea. At the time it was called the Hermit Kingdom and they made their way across secretly to come and trade. They weren't allowed to do that. It was illegal. They didn't like that in the Hermit Kingdom. North Korea or Korea as we know it now, North Korea now, and these people came and they met this man, Robert Germain Thomas, and they began to speak to him and he began to realize these people spoke a different language and he offered them a copy of the Chinese scripture. They said, where did you get that? He said, I have many of these Bibles. And he would go on secret mission trips with these two, a couple of people from the Hermit Kingdom, and he would deliver the scripture secretly. He'd go in and out, in and out. Eventually one day he had this burden. He wanted to go into the Hermit Kingdom and he wanted to give the Bible to the leaders and he had this desire and this American captain kind of used him and he went in and it all went a little bit wrong. The Hermit Kingdom thought they were attacking them and so they began to open fire upon these people and so he got off the boat and he began to throw as many copies of God's Word as he possibly could. He got onto the shores of the Hermit Kingdom. There was a man there and if you met someone who was an outsider, you were compelled to execute them on the spot. Robert Germain Thomas stood on the shores of Korea and he said, here's a Bible. It's just a Bible. The man from the Hermit Kingdom took his sword and he executed him on the spot. What they did in those days, the leaders took the trophies of their victims and they took the Bible, didn't really know what it was, and they tore it apart and they took the pages and they put them on the wall of the house, of his house, sort of a wallpaper decoration. One by one the locals began to come in and read the Scriptures and people were converted.
